sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Yasuichiro Yamamoto keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and mystery, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Case Closed: The Fist of Blue Sapphire, Howl's Moving Castle—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky (2004) is best described as Animation, Mystery, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: Magician of the Silver Sky part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
